svg.title: Five-question design-review checklist for genomics ML projects
svg.desc: A two-column reference card. The left column lists five design-review questions with example diagnostics: architecture match, likelihood match, split protocol, baseline strength, and calibration plus error analysis. The right column lists, for each question, the typical failure mode the question is meant to prevent. A header summarises the move; a footer points to the relevant chapter sections for each question.
svg.text: GENOMICS ML DESIGN REVIEW — FIVE QUESTIONS, FIVE FAILURE MODES
Run before the experiment; re-run before trusting another paper's headline number.

g.text: Does the architecture's inductive bias match the data's structural property?
2-D image → CNN. Long 1-D + sparse range → dilated conv + attention.
Counts → VAE with discrete likelihood. Graph → message-passing GNN. 3-D → equivariant attention.
Mismatched prior — 10 — 100 × more data needed
Transformer on a small pileup task, MLP on protein structure, vanilla CNN on a graph.
Is the output likelihood matched to the target distribution?
Gaussian for continuous; categorical cross-entropy for discrete; Poisson for low counts;
negative binomial for over-dispersed counts; ZINB for over-dispersed + zero-inflated.
Wrong likelihood — silent statistical bias
MSE on counts; Gaussian on heavy-tailed data; ignoring zero-inflation in 10×-Chromium data.
Does the train / test split control for the dataset's correlation structure?
Cluster splits for protein tasks (CD-HIT / MMseqs2); chromosome splits for regulation;
kinship-aware splits for biobanks; leave-one-study-out for cross-study single-cell.
Data leakage — inflated 20 — 30 pp on the headline number
Random 80/20 on homologous proteins; per-SNP random splits; ignoring kinship in biobanks.
Is the best available specialised baseline included in the comparison?
For cells: scVI, Harmony, CellTypist. For regulation: Basenji / Enformer. For variants: GATK + DeepVariant.
Same data, same split, same evaluation metric — not a hand-picked easy comparator.
Weak-baseline win — apparent improvement is comparator-induced
Beating a logistic-regression baseline; beating an outdated specialised tool; no baseline at all.
Is the model calibrated and is its error analysed across sub-populations?
Reliability diagram; expected calibration error; stratified accuracy by ancestry, batch, cell type.
A clinically useful model needs more than one accuracy number on a homogeneous test set.
Mis-calibrated or sub-group failures hidden
Confident wrong answers; ancestry-biased GWAS PRS; poor accuracy on rare cell types.

svg.text: Most genomics ML controversies reduce to a failure on one of these five questions.
